summary: Iteration-20 real-MoreWine PDF ingestion + sparkling grounding order: end-to-end PASS across DB, engine, Cellar Brief cards, and mobile UI. Backend pytest 10/10 new iter-20 tests + 27/27 iter-19/18/16 regression = 37/37. Direct DB proofs: (1) diy_knowledge_chunks has all 16 morew_* source_docs from the requested list — morew_bench_trials (3), morew_fining_agents (6), morew_inert_gas (6), morew_mlf_paper (4), morew_oak_barrel_care (8), morew_oak_info (4), morew_oxygen_ferment (8), morew_ph_meter (3), morew_red_outline (7), morew_sanitation (2), morew_so2_mgmt (10), morew_so2_protocol (20), morew_sparkling_proelif (3), morew_sparkling_yeast (6), morew_yeast_hydration (3), morew_yeast_pairing (20). morew_white_outline table row count = 0 (fully dropped). (2) Sparkling docs tagged at WBS 9.1/9.3/9.4: morew_sparkling_yeast has 6 rows (2 per WBS × p1+p2), morew_sparkling_proelif has 3 rows (1 per WBS), total = 9 sparkling chunks. (3) Content quality — WBS 9.3 morew_sparkling_yeast chunk includes real MoreWine numerics: 'pH>2.9', 'FS02<15ppm', '~5% of the wine volume', '70-100x106 active yeast cells/mL', '1-1.5x106 active yeast cells/mL', 'Alc. ≤11.5%', 'temperature>12°C/54°F'. Backend API proofs (tRPC v11 with body {json:{}}): (a) /api/trpc/cellarBrief.latest — Tank 20 (variety='Sparkling Chardonnay Cuvée') grounding array is exactly ['SOP 13 White Wine Fermentation Temperature Control', 'SOP 39 Stuck Fermentation — Diagnosis & Restart', 'Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast p1 — SPARKLING WINE PROTOCOL', 'Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast p2 — Page 2']. Zero red-flow chips (no 'Red Wine Fermentation', 'Pump-Over', 'Cap Management', 'Red Wine Bible'). ghostQuestion.category='sparkling', journalSlug='sparkling-bubbles-when-should-i-press-sparkling-base-fruit-if-i-want-the-brightest-cleanest-wine'. (b) Tank 2 Chardonnay (still white) grounding = ['SOP 13 White Wine Fermentation Temperature Control', 'SOP 17 Yeast Nutrition & DAP Additions', 'White Wine Bible Ch.3 — Add the Yeast and Begin Fermentation', 'Yeast & Grape Pairing p1 — Page 1'] — White Wine Bible D-prefix chunk makes the 2-chunk cap ahead of the specialist manual (Yeast & Grape Pairing chip is fine per spec). Tank 3 Chardonnay grounding contains 'White Wine Bible Ch.9.6' + 'Inert Gas in Winemaking p1'. Zero sparkling or red leakage on still whites. (c) Red-variety cards (Tank 1/3/9 Shiraz, Tank 8 Tempranillo, Tank 4 Grenache, Tank 5 Cabernet Sauvignon, Tank 2 Merlot) all have 'SOP 11 Red Wine Fermentation' + 'SOP 12 Pump-Over Protocol' + 'SOP 14 Cap Management' + 'Red Wine Bible intro' + 'Red Wine Bible Ch.3'. Zero sparkling or white leakage. Tank 4 Pinot Noir MLF has 'SOP 21 MLF Management' + 'SOP 22 MLF Inoculation' + 'Red Wine Bible Ch.6/Ch.10.5'. (d) cellar_journal COUNT(*) = 307 (>=305). /api/sitemap.xml returns HTTP 200 application/xml with 308 <loc> tags. Frontend Playwright PASS on /cellar-brief mobile 430×1400: Tank 20 sparkling card renders end-to-end with TRACKING pill, 'Sparkling Chardonnay Cuvée' label, 'Primary slowing · day 8' stage chip, 'Brix 2.5 · ferment finishing (2.8°Bx/day)' trajectory, DECISION DUE banner 'Press window open — confirm dryness + bench taste before pressing', Today's Work checklist ('Daily Brix to confirm <1°Bx before pressing/racking', 'Taste sample — confirm sugars consumed'), GROUNDED IN chips visible in the card body (SOP 13, SOP 39, Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast p1 — SPARKLING WINE PROTOCOL, Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast p2 — Page 2), Worth Knowing expander with question 'When should I press sparkling base fruit if I want the brightest, cleanest wine?' + Tasmania/Jansz/Clover Hill/House of Arras/Yarra Valley/Great Southern WA in the answer, and 'Read full answer + citations →' link data-testid='brief-ghost-readmore-tank-20-sparkling-chardonnay-cuv-e' href='/cellar-journal/sparkling-bubbles-when-should-i-press-sparkling-base-fruit-if-i-want-the-brightest-cleanest-wine?from=cellar-brief'. Journal index at /cellar-journal shows '307 ENTRIES' with SPARKLING & BUBBLES · 54 topic chip and full topic breakdown.
action_items: (Carry-over from iter_18) cellarJournal.list rows still omit `source` and `teaserAnswer` from the SELECT — detail-only fields via getBySlug. Not blocking; only spec-vs-impl drift.

(Carry-over from iter_16) cellarBrief.history pagination via beforeGeneratedAt cursor still has the duplicate wineryId AND clause instead of lt(generatedAt, cursor). Not exercised in iter-20 but noted.

(Nit) Review-request mentioned '15 REAL MoreWine PDFs' but listed 16 source_docs (including morew_red_outline which pre-existed from prior iter). Ingestion count of 106 new rows and drop of morew_white_outline both verified — nothing to fix, just spec vs. reality reconciliation.
critical_code_review_comments: cellarBriefEngine.ts STAGE_TO_WBS ordering with 9.x codes first for sparkling and D-prefix codes first for whites is working exactly as designed — Tank 20 grounding puts the two Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast chunks after the two SOP chips (SOP 13, SOP 39) within the 4-chip cap and Tank 2 Chardonnay puts White Wine Bible Ch.3 in the cap ahead of the Yeast & Grape Pairing manual.

ingest-morewine-pdfs.py chunking + WBS tagging looks correct: sparkling-yeast tagged at 9.1/9.3/9.4 with 2 pages each (6 rows total), sparkling-proelif at 9.1/9.3/9.4 with 1 page each (3 rows total). Both docs surface via the SOURCE_LABEL map as 'Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast' / 'Sparkling Wine Protocol — ProElif'. Only 'Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast' is showing on Tank 20 — ProElif is not being pulled, likely because morew_sparkling_yeast is preferred first in the sparkling branch (as intended per the review-request description).

Zero cross-contamination across all 14 Cellar Brief cards: no 'Red Wine Bible' on any white/sparkling card; no 'Sparkling Wine Protocol' on any still-red card; no 'White Wine Bible' on any red card. SPECIALIST_MANUALS augmentation is only applied when the surface allows — Tank 2 Chardonnay (white, nutrition context) got 'Yeast & Grape Pairing', Tank 3 Chardonnay got 'Inert Gas in Winemaking', Tank 7 Shiraz got 'SO₂ Management' — all colour-correct.

Data quality: WBS 9.3 sparkling yeast content preserved all key MoreWine numeric protocol markers (pH>2.9, FSO2<15ppm, 5% starter, 70–100×10⁶ cells/mL, 1–1.5×10⁶ inoculum, ≤11.5% ABV, ≥12°C). PDF extraction artefacts noted in the review-request are acknowledged but do not affect grounding readability.

Regression footprint clean: journal count still 307 (was 307 in iter-19), sitemap still 308 <loc>, all iter-19 sparkling tests + iter-18 coherence tests + iter-16 cellar-brief tests remain green (27/27).

success_rate.backend: 100% (37/37 pytest: 10 new iter-20 morewine + 11 iter-19 sparkling + 8 iter-18 coherence + 8 iter-16 cellar-brief regression)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (Tank 20 sparkling card renders end-to-end with correct Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast chips in GROUNDED IN, Worth Knowing expander, Read full answer + citations link with sparkling-bubbles-* href; /cellar-journal index shows 307 ENTRIES with SPARKLING & BUBBLES · 54 chip)

test_credentials: Auth bypass ON (ENABLE_DEV_BYPASS, seed-owner-001 = winery_id=1). No login required.
seed_data_creation: None. Consumed existing 106 morew_* diy_knowledge_chunks rows inserted by the main-agent /app/scripts/ingest-morewine-pdfs.py run; verified morew_white_outline drop was completed (0 rows).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-20 wired real MoreWine PDF content into the diy_knowledge_chunks corpus (106 new rows across 15 source_docs plus the pre-existing morew_red_outline making 16 morew_* docs total). The mislabelled morew_white_outline (7 LLM-synth rows) was fully dropped. Sparkling grounding on Tank 20 now surfaces 'Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast p1/p2' chips ahead of any specialist manual, while still-white cards keep White Wine Bible D-prefix chunks in their 2-chunk cap. Every colour route was verified for zero cross-contamination: no red bibles on whites/sparkling, no sparkling protocols on reds, no white bibles on reds. Specialist manuals (yeast pairing, inert gas, SO2, MLF, oak) surface as colour-appropriate 5th chips when the vessel stage matches. Cache was invalidated during main-agent testing so cellarBrief.latest regenerated live. When adding future ingestion runs, always: (i) preserve SOURCE_LABEL entries for readable chip titles, (ii) tag both a primary WBS and secondary WBS codes via duplicated rows, (iii) keep the sparkling branch's preferred[] starting with the sparkling-specific docs to guarantee they win the 2-chunk cap. Journal list is still server-capped at limit=60 and the top-level key is `rows` (not `items`). tRPC v11 GET input is url-encoded {json: {...}}.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All 9 verification points from the review request pass end-to-end: (1) 16 morew_* source_docs including all 15 requested, morew_white_outline confirmed at 0 rows; (2) sparkling chunks tagged across WBS 9.1/9.3/9.4 in both morew_sparkling_yeast (6 rows) and morew_sparkling_proelif (3 rows), total 9; (3) real MoreWine numerics preserved in WBS 9.3 yeast chunk (pH>2.9, FSO2<15ppm, 5% starter volume, 70–100×10⁶ cells/mL); (4) Tank 20 grounding contains 'Sparkling Wine Protocol — Yeast p1/p2' chips with zero red-flow leakage; (5) Tank 2 & Tank 3 Chardonnay still show White Wine Bible chips within the cap with colour-correct specialist manual companions; (6) red-variety cards keep full red-flow SOP + Red Wine Bible stack with zero sparkling leakage; (7) mobile 430×1400 Tank 20 UI renders with all expected sub-elements including the Read-full-answer deep link to /cellar-journal/sparkling-bubbles-*?from=cellar-brief; (8) journal count 307 (>=305), sitemap 308 <loc>; (9) all iter-16/17/18/19 regression tests remain green.
